chief_complaint: A 50-year-old right-handed male presents with focal right-sided upper limb and facial seizures.
history: The patient has type 2 diabetes mellitus and presents with complaints of focal seizures, acalculia, finger agnosia, left-right disorientation, semantic aphasia, and loss of comprehension. He has no significant past medical history and is not on any medications.
exam: Vital signs: BP 120/80 mmHg, HR 70 bpm, RR 16 breaths/min. Physical examination reveals focal right-sided upper limb and facial weakness.
labs: Laboratory reports: Blood glucose 350 mg/dL, sodium 145 mmol/L, potassium 3.5 mmol/L. MRI of the brain reveals dominant (left) parietal lobe pathology.
correct_diagnosis: Gerstmann syndrome associated with hyperglycemic seizures
differentials: Gerstmann syndrome associated with hyperglycemic seizures
Focal seizures due to parietal lobe pathology
correct_ranking: Gerstmann syndrome associated with hyperglycemic seizures
Focal seizures due to parietal lobe pathology
